generatedSentence: A carefree loner, known for deejaying and his habit of eating nachos galore, enjoyed a typical workday under his aluminum canopy, unknowingly holding a certificate of profound origins. He didn't think much of his low-value life, often layback in his armchair, playing gamepad, his only consistency being his idiotic, sappy greeting to the stray cats by his window. Suddenly, a terrible flashpoint occurred: paparazzi, rocketing in, grabbed his elder; a telltale electron shell, a gift from his womb, was demanded by the ruthless mafia, turning his carefree existence into a numbing deadline. He realized his mission was no longer a game, but an intractable conflict, a savage outrush of despair. Our patriot gumshoe, blaster in hand, navigates the outskirt bars, a trifecta of kinks and blotchy, unsanitary interiors, searching for the bastard who denied his nationality a fair treat. He battles a homunculus, its copper form shining under the sundeck's canopy, royally disallowing any retreat, a sinking feeling matching the torrents of habanero he feels. He secured the receipt, a value equally handy as his netbook, finally allowing him to capture the phonily grieving loser, whose vane efforts to handicap him had created a sore, demanding situation. The arboreal botany near the fairway, where a lone killdeer nested, served as a quiet reminder as he took a final sampling, ready to incubate a new beginning at midnight.

He didn't think much of his low-value life, often layback in his armchair, playing gamepad, his only consistency being his idiotic, sappy greeting to the stray cats by his window.

Suddenly, a terrible flashpoint occurred: paparazzi, rocketing in, grabbed his elder; a telltale electron shell, a gift from his womb, was demanded by the ruthless mafia, turning his carefree existence into a numbing deadline. He realized his mission was no longer a game, but an intractable conflict, a savage outrush of despair.

He secured the receipt, a value equally handy as his netbook, finally allowing him to capture the phonily grieving loser, whose vane efforts to handicap him had created a sore, demanding situation. The arboreal botany near the fairway, where a lone killdeer nested, served as a quiet reminder as he took a final sampling, ready to incubate a new beginning at midnight.

He didn't think much of his low-value life, often layback in his armchair, playing gamepad, his only consistency being his idiotic, sappy greeting to the stray cats by his window.

Suddenly, a terrible flashpoint occurred: paparazzi, rocketing in, grabbed his elder; a telltale electron shell, a gift from his womb, was demanded by the ruthless mafia, turning his carefree existence into a numbing deadline. He realized his mission was no longer a game, but an intractable conflict, a savage outrush of despair.

Our patriot gumshoe, blaster in hand, navigates the outskirt bars, a trifecta of kinks and blotchy, unsanitary interiors, searching for the bastard who denied his nationality a fair treat. He battles a homunculus, its copper form shining under the sundeck's canopy, royally disallowing any retreat, a sinking feeling matching the torrents of habanero he feels.

He secured the receipt, a value equally handy as his netbook, finally allowing him to capture the phonily grieving loser, whose vane efforts to handicap him had created a sore, demanding situation. The arboreal botany near the fairway, where a lone killdeer nested, served as a quiet reminder as he took a final sampling, ready to incubate a new beginning at midnight.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A cyber-noir gumshoe, wearing a jade-green trench coat and a gleaming aluminum fedora. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The gumshoe, relaxed in an armchair, typing on a netbook, an unseen window showing a neon city outskirt, low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Paparazzi's flashbulbs explode as a shadowy figure, the 'elder', is grabbed by a mafia enforcer, dynamic medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The gumshoe, blaster raised, battles a small, copper-skinned homunculus in a shadowy bar, dramatic close-up. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The gumshoe holds up a glowing receipt, standing victorious over a phonily weeping loser, an outskirt fairway in the background, wide shot.

definitions.definition: The tree of the species Citrus paradisi, a hybrid of pomelo (Citrus maxima) and sweet orange.
The large spherical tart fruit produced by this tree.
An abundance; plenty.
In abundance.
definitions.example: After the shipwreck there was whisky galore to be had for the taking.

definitions.definition: A document containing a certified statement.
A document evidencing ownership or debt.
A document serving as evidence as a person has completed an educational course, issued either by an institution not authorised to grant diplomas, or to a student not qualifying for a diploma.
The information needed in order to verify a positive answer to a problem.
A motion picture age rating.
definitions.example: The film is certificate 15.

definitions.definition: To supply with a certificate, especially following certification
(heading, physical) To move or be moved into something.
(heading, social) To diminish or be diminished.
To conceal and appropriate.
To keep out of sight; to suppress; to ignore.
To pay absolutely.
definitions.example: I have sunk thousands of pounds into this project.

definitions.definition: To reduce or extinguish by payment.
definitions.example: to sink the national debt

definitions.definition: To be overwhelmed or depressed; to fail in strength.
To decrease in volume, as a river; to subside; to become diminished in volume or in apparent height.
The process by which something sinks.
definitions.example: I witnessed the sinking of my ship from the shore.

definitions.definition: A paparazzo.
Paparazzi taken as a group.
A contest, a struggle.
definitions.example: the battle of life

definitions.definition: A general action, fight, or encounter, in which all the divisions of an army are or may be engaged; a combat, an engagement.
A division of an army; a battalion.
The main body of an army, as distinct from the vanguard and rear; the battalia.
To join in battle; to contend in fight
definitions.example: Scientists always battle over theories.

definitions.definition: To fight or struggle; to enter into a battle with.
definitions.example: She has been battling cancer for years.

definitions.definition: To nourish; feed.
To render (for example soil) fertile or fruitful
An act of thinking; consideration (of something).
definitions.example: I'll have a think about that and let you know.

definitions.definition: To ponder, to go over in one's head.
definitions.example: Idly, the detective thought what his next move should be.

definitions.definition: To communicate to oneself in one's mind, to try to find a solution to a problem.
definitions.example: I thought for three hours about the problem and still couldn’t find the solution.

definitions.definition: To conceive of something or someone (usually followed by of; infrequently, by on).
definitions.example: I tend to think of her as rather ugly.

definitions.definition: To be of opinion (that); to consider, judge, regard, or look upon (something) as.
definitions.example: At the time I thought his adamant refusal to give in right.

definitions.definition: To guess; to reckon.
definitions.example: I think she’ll pass the examination.

definitions.definition: To plan; to be considering; to be of a mind (to do something).
To presume; to venture.
An entertainment, outing, food, drink, or other indulgence provided by someone for the enjoyment of others.
definitions.example: Here are some healthy Halloween treats for ghouls and witches of all ages.

definitions.definition: An unexpected gift, event etc., which provides great pleasure.
definitions.example: It was such a treat to see her back in action on the London stage.

definitions.definition: A snack food item designed to be given to pets.
definitions.example: I lured the cat into her carrier by throwing a couple of treats in there.

definitions.definition: A parley or discussion of terms; a negotiation.
An entreaty.
To negotiate, discuss terms, bargain (for or with).
To discourse; to handle a subject in writing or speaking; to conduct a discussion.
definitions.example: Cicero's writing treats mainly of old age and personal duty.

definitions.definition: To discourse on; to represent or deal with in a particular way, in writing or speaking.
definitions.example: The article treated feminism as a quintessentially modern movement.

definitions.definition: To entreat or beseech (someone).
definitions.example: Only let my family live, I treat thee.

definitions.definition: To handle, deal with or behave towards in a specific way.
definitions.example: She was tempted to treat the whole affair as a joke.

definitions.definition: To entertain with food or drink, especially at one's own expense; to show hospitality to; to pay for as celebration or reward.
definitions.example: I treated my son to some popcorn in the interval.

definitions.definition: To commit the offence of providing food, drink, entertainment or provision to corruptly influence a voter.
To care for medicinally or surgically; to apply medical care to.
definitions.example: They treated me for malaria.

definitions.definition: To subject to a chemical or other action; to act upon with a specific scientific result in mind.
definitions.example: He treated the substance with sulphuric acid.

definitions.definition: To provide something special and pleasant.
An action performed on a regular basis.
definitions.example: It’s become a habit of mine to have a cup of coffee after dinner.

definitions.definition: An action performed repeatedly and automatically, usually without awareness.
definitions.example: By force of habit, he dressed for work even though it was holiday.

definitions.definition: A long piece of clothing worn by monks and nuns.
definitions.example: It’s interesting how Catholic and Buddhist monks both wear habits.

definitions.definition: A piece of clothing worn uniformly for a specific activity.
definitions.example: The new riding habits of the team looked smashing!

definitions.definition: Outward appearance; attire; dress.
Form of growth or general appearance of a variety or species of plant or crystal.
An addiction.
definitions.example: He has a 10-cigar habit.

definitions.definition: Without cares or worries; free of concern or worries; without difficulty.
definitions.example: He left his cell phone behind and enjoyed a carefree summer day at the beach.

definitions.definition: A weather vane

Any of several usually relatively thin, rigid, flat, or sometimes curved surfaces radially mounted along an axis, as a blade in a turbine or a sail on a windmill, that is turned by or used to turn a fluid
The flattened, web-like part of a feather, consisting of a series of barbs on either side of the shaft
A sight on a sextant or compass
One of the metal guidance or stabilizing fins attached to the tail of a bomb or other missile
Membership of a particular nation or state, by origin, birth, naturalization, ownership, allegiance or otherwise.
National, i.e. ethnic and/or cultural, character or identity.
A people sharing a common origin, culture and/or language, and possibly constituting a nation-state.
Political existence, independence or unity as a national entity.
Nationalism or patriotism.
(of a person or animal) Pertaining to or resembling an idiot; characterised with behaviour resembling idiocy.
(of an action) Having the quality of idiocy; very foolish
The lowest temperature at which a liquid can form an ignitable mixture in air near the surface of the liquid.
A hotspot; a dangerous place of violent political unrest
A time and place when violence or conflict suddenly arises.
So as to cause terror or awe.
definitions.example: The lion roared terribly.

definitions.definition: Very; extremely.
definitions.example: He's terribly busy and you really shouldn't bother him.

definitions.definition: Very badly.
definitions.example: She took part in the karaoke, but sang terribly.

definitions.definition: Elongated portion.
Connected sequence of people or things.
To practice an ability.
definitions.example: She trained seven hours a day to prepare for the Olympics.

definitions.definition: To teach and form (someone) by practice; to educate (someone).
definitions.example: You can't train a pig to write poetry.

definitions.definition: To improve one's fitness.
definitions.example: I trained with weights all winter.

definitions.definition: To proceed in sequence.
To move (a gun) laterally so that it points in a different direction.
definitions.example: The assassin had trained his gun on the minister.

definitions.definition: To encourage (a plant or branch) to grow in a particular direction or shape, usually by pruning and bending.
definitions.example: The vine had been trained over the pergola.

definitions.definition: To trace (a lode or any mineral appearance) to its head.
To create a trainer for; to apply cheats to (a game).
To draw along; to trail; to drag.
To draw by persuasion, artifice, or the like; to attract by stratagem; to entice; to allure.
A violent flow, as of water, lava, etc.; a stream suddenly raised and running rapidly, as down a precipice.
definitions.example: A torrent of green and white water broke over the hull of the sail-boat.

definitions.definition: A large amount or stream of something.
definitions.example: They endured a torrent of inquiries.

definitions.definition: (file sharing) A set of files obtainable through a peer-to-peer network, especially BitTorrent.
definitions.example: I got a torrent of the complete works of Shakespeare the other day; I'm not sure why.

definitions.definition: A sequence of rhythmic steps or movements usually performed to music, for pleasure or as a form of social interaction.
A social gathering where dancing is the main activity.
A normally horizontal stripe called a fess that has been modified to zig-zag across the center of a coat of arms from dexter to sinister.
A genre of modern music characterised by sampled beats, repetitive rhythms and few lyrics.
The art, profession, and study of dancing.
A piece of music with a particular dance rhythm.
A battle of wits, especially one commonly fought between two rivals.
definitions.example: So how much longer are we gonna do this dance?

definitions.definition: To move with rhythmic steps or movements, especially in time to music.
definitions.example: I danced with her all night long.

definitions.definition: To leap or move lightly and rapidly.
definitions.example: His eyes danced with pleasure as he spoke.   She accused her political opponent of dancing around the issue instead of confronting it.

definitions.definition: To perform the steps to.
definitions.example: Have you ever danced the tango?

definitions.definition: To cause to dance, or move nimbly or merrily about.
To make love or have sex.
definitions.example: You make me feel like dancing.

definitions.definition: A single tortilla chip from a dish of nachos.
A Mexican dish of tortilla chips, sometimes covered in melted cheese.
Anything that, or anyone who blasts.
definitions.example: infrared blaster

definitions.definition: A device to detonate an explosive, often consisting of a box with a handle to be pressed down.
An unspecified powerful hand weapon, usually one that fires an energy pulse or beam.
A sand wedge.
To grip suddenly; to seize; to clutch.
definitions.example: I grabbed her hand to pull her back from the cliff edge.

definitions.definition: To make a sudden grasping or clutching motion (at something).
definitions.example: The suspect suddenly broke free and grabbed at the policeman's gun.

definitions.definition: To restrain someone; to arrest.
To grip the attention of; to enthrall or interest.
definitions.example: How does that idea grab you?

definitions.definition: To quickly collect or retrieve.
To consume something quickly.
definitions.example: Is there time to grab a coffee?

definitions.definition: To take the opportunity of.
Excessively sweet, emotional, nostalgic; cheesy; mushy. (British equivalent: soppy)
definitions.example: It was a sappy love song, but it reminded them of their first dance.

definitions.definition: Having (a particularly large amount of) sap.
Juicy.
(of wood) Spongy; Having spaces in which large quantities of sap can flow.
A semiprecious stone, either nephrite or jadeite, generally green or white in color, often used for carving figurines.
A bright shade of slightly bluish or greyish green, typical of polished jade stones.
A succulent plant, Crassula ovata.
Of a grayish shade of green, typical of jade stones.
To accelerate swiftly and powerfully
To fly vertically
To rise or soar rapidly
To carry something in a rocket
To attack something with rockets
A bet in which the bettor must select the first three placegetters of a race in the order in which they finish.
The attainment of three important achievements, qualities, etc.
(by extension) A set of three related things, often things that cause problems.
definitions.example: 2011, Beatriz Scaglia, The Trifecta of Evil and the Crisis of Faith: Devil, Satan, and Lucifer.

definitions.definition: (US politics) The complete legislative and executive control of state government by one party: governorship, the upper house, and the lower house of state legislatures. The party that holds all three positions of power is said to have a trifecta.
(US politics) Control by one party of the federal government: the presidency and both houses of Congress.
One who trespasses; an interloper.
To cause to become numb (physically or emotionally).
definitions.example: The dentist gave me novocaine to numb my tooth before drilling, thank goodness.

definitions.definition: To cause (a feeling) to be less intense.
definitions.example: He turned to alcohol to numb his pain.

definitions.definition: To cause (the mind, faculties, etc.) to be less acute.
To become numb (especially physically).
An act by which something is numbed.
A convulsive fit of coughing or laughter; a sonorous indraft of breath; a whoop; a gasp of breath caused by laughing, coughing, or crying.
To laugh loudly.
To gasp for breath as in a severe fit of coughing.
A tight curl, twist, or bend in a length of thin material, hair etc.
definitions.example: We couldn't get enough water to put out the fire because of a kink in the hose.

definitions.definition: A difficulty or flaw that is likely to impede operation, as in a plan or system.
definitions.example: They had planned to open another shop downtown, but their plan had a few kinks.

definitions.definition: An unreasonable notion; a crotchet; a whim; a caprice.
Peculiarity or deviation in sexual behaviour or taste.
A positive 1-soliton solution to the Sine–Gordon equation
To form a kink or twist.
To be formed into a kink or twist.
A hierarchically structured secret organisation engaged in illegal activities like distribution of narcotics, gambling and extortion.
A crime syndicate.
A trusted group of associates, as of a political leader.
(in compound terms such as "moral mafia") An entity which attempts to control a specified arena.
definitions.example: the digital mafia

definitions.definition: A person who loses; one who fails to win or thrive.
definitions.example: He was always a good loser.

definitions.definition: Something of poor quality.
A person who is frequently unsuccessful in life.
definitions.example: I'm a constant loser in love.

definitions.definition: A contemptible or unfashionable person.
One who or that which loses something, such as extra weight, car keys, etc.
To perform as a disc jockey.
Not tractable; not able to be managed, controlled, governed or directed.
(of a mathematical problem) Not able to be solved.
(of a problem) Difficult to deal with, solve, or manage.
(of a person) Stubborn; obstinate.
Difficult to treat (of a medical condition).
An intensely spicy chili pepper, Capsicum chinense.
A miniature man, once imagined by spermists to be present in human sperm.
The nerve map of the human body that exists on the parietal lobe of the human brain.
An uncivilized or feral human; a barbarian.
A defiant person.
To attack or assault someone or something ferociously or without restraint.
definitions.example: No matter how anyone might savage me, I should stay strong.

definitions.definition: To criticise vehemently.
definitions.example: His latest film was savaged by most reviewers.

definitions.definition: (of an animal) To attack with the teeth.
To make savage.
Wild; not cultivated.
definitions.example: a savage wilderness

definitions.definition: Barbaric; not civilized.
definitions.example: savage manners

definitions.definition: Fierce and ferocious.
definitions.example: a savage spirit

definitions.definition: Brutal, vicious or merciless.
definitions.example: He gave the dog a savage kick.

definitions.definition: Unpleasant or unfair.
Great, brilliant, amazing.
definitions.example: Although it didn't look very good, it tasted absolutely savage.

definitions.definition: Nude; naked.
One who divulges private information with intent to hurt others.
Tattletale; squealer.
An indicator, such as a warning light, that serves to warn of a hazard or problem.
Something that serves to reveal something else.
definitions.example: The telltale was the lipstick on his shirt collar.

definitions.definition: A movable piece of ivory, lead, or other material, connected to the bellows of an organ, whose position indicates when the wind is exhausted.
A length of yarn or ribbon attached to a sail or shroud etc to indicate the direction of the flow of the air relative to the boat.
A mechanical attachment to the steering wheel, which, in the absence of a tiller, shows the position of the helm.
A compass in the cabin of a vessel, usually placed where the captain can see it at all hours, and thus inform himself of the vessel's course.

A machine or contrivance for indicating or recording something, particularly for keeping a check upon employees (factory hands, watchmen, drivers, etc.) by revealing to their employers what they have done or omitted.
A bird, the tattler.
A story or fable that has a moral or message.
Revealing something, especially something not intended to be known.
definitions.example: He blushed when he approached, a telltale sign that he was happy to see him.

definitions.definition: To request forcefully.
definitions.example: I demand to see the manager.

definitions.definition: To claim a right to something.
definitions.example: The bank is demanding the mortgage payment.

definitions.definition: To ask forcefully for information.
definitions.example: I demand an immediate explanation.

definitions.definition: To require of someone.
definitions.example: This job demands a lot of patience.

definitions.definition: To issue a summons to court.
Requiring much endurance, strength, or patience.
Loss of hope; utter hopelessness; complete despondency.
definitions.example: He turned around in despair, aware that he was not going to survive

definitions.definition: That which causes despair.
That which is despaired of.
To give up as beyond hope or expectation; to despair of.
To cause to despair.
(often with “of”) To be hopeless; to have no hope; to give up all hope or expectation.
A time limit in the form of a date on or before which something must be completed.
definitions.example: I must make this deadline or my boss will kill me!

definitions.definition: A guideline marked on a plate for a printing press.
A line that does not move.
A boundary around a prison, prisoners crossing which would be shot.
To render an item non-mission-capable; to ground an aircraft, etc.
In a phony way, or to a phony extent
A person who was born out of wedlock, and hence often considered an illegitimate descendant.
A mongrel (biological cross between different breeds, groups or varieties).
(typically referring to a man) A contemptible, inconsiderate, overly or arrogantly rude or spiteful person.
definitions.example: Some bastard stole my car while I was helping an injured person.

definitions.definition: A man, a fellow, a male friend.
definitions.example: Get over here, you old bastard!

definitions.definition: (often preceded by 'poor') A person deserving of pity.
definitions.example: Poor bastard, I feel so sorry for him.

definitions.definition: A child who does not know his or her father.
Something extremely difficult or unpleasant to deal with.
definitions.example: Life can be a real bastard.

definitions.definition: A variation that is not genuine; something irregular or inferior or of dubious origin, fake or counterfeit.
definitions.example: The architecture was a kind of bastard, suggesting Gothic but not being true Gothic.

definitions.definition: An intermediate-grade file; also bastard file.
A sweet wine.
A sword that is midway in length between a short-sword and a long sword; also bastard sword.
An inferior quality of soft brown sugar, obtained from syrups that have been boiled several times.
A large mould for straining sugar.
A writing paper of a particular size.
A Eurosceptic Conservative MP, especially in the government of John Major.
To bastardize.
Of or like a bastard (illegitimate human descendant).
Of or like a bastard (bad person).
Of or like a mongrel, bastardized creature/cross.
Of abnormal, irregular or otherwise inferior qualities (size, shape etc).
definitions.example: a bastard culverin

definitions.definition: Spurious, lacking authenticity: counterfeit, fake.
Used in the vernacular name of a species to indicate that it is similar in some way to another species, often (but not always) one of another genus.
definitions.example: bastard gemsbok

definitions.definition: Very unpleasant.
definitions.example: I've got a bastard headache.

definitions.definition: Abbreviated, as the half title in a page preceding the full title page of a book.
(theater lighting) Consisting of one predominant color blended with small amounts of complementary color; used to replicate natural light because of their warmer appearance.
definitions.example: A bastard orange gel produces predominantly orange light with undertones of blue.

definitions.definition: Exclamation of strong dismay or strong sense of being upset.
A metallic chemical element (symbol Al) with an atomic number of 13.
A single atom of this element.
Aircraft or other machinery made partially or wholly of aluminum.
Suitable for everyday use.
Mundane or commonplace.

Any of the days of a week on which work is done. The five workdays in many countries are usually Monday to Friday (and are defined as such in official and legal usage even though many people work on weekends).
definitions.example: It will take five workdays to process your application.

definitions.definition: That part of a day in which work is done.
definitions.example: My workday is 8 hours.

definitions.definition: A high cover providing shelter, such as a cloth supported above an object, particularly over a bed.
Any overhanging or projecting roof structure, typically over entrances or doors.
The zone of the highest foliage and branches of a forest.
In an airplane, the transparent cockpit cover.
In a parachute, the cloth that fills with air and thus limits the falling speed.
To cover with or as if with a canopy.
To go through the canopy of a forest on a zipline.
Local coherence.
Correspondence or compatibility.
Reliability or uniformity; the quality of being consistent.
definitions.example: They want to achieve a high degree of consistency in their process and their product.

definitions.definition: The degree of viscosity of something.
definitions.example: Mix it until it has the consistency of a thick paste.

definitions.definition: Freedom from contradiction; the state of a system of axioms such that none of the propositions deduced from them are mutually contradictory.
Firmness of constitution or character; substantiality; durability; persistency.
Any of a group of European plants, genus, Lavandula, of the mint family.
A pale purple colour, like that of the lavender flower.
To decorate or perfume with lavender.
Having a pale purple colour.
Pertaining to LGBT people and rights
Pertaining to lesbian feminism; opposing heterosexism.
Something that is low; a low point.
definitions.example: Economic growth has hit a new low.

definitions.definition: The minimum value attained by some quantity within a specified period.
definitions.example: Unemployment has reached a ten-year low.

definitions.definition: A depressed mood or situation.
definitions.example: He is in a low right now

definitions.definition: An area of low pressure; a depression.
definitions.example: A deep low is centred over the British Isles.

definitions.definition: The lowest-speed gearing of a power-transmission system, especially of an automotive vehicle.
definitions.example: Shift out of low before the car gets to eight miles per hour.

definitions.definition: The lowest trump, usually the deuce; the lowest trump dealt or drawn.
(slang) (usually accompanied by "the") a cheap, cost-efficient, or advantageous payment or expense.
definitions.example: He got the brand new Yankees jersey for the low.

definitions.definition: To depress; to lower.
Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane; not high or lofty.
definitions.example: a low wall  a low shelf

definitions.definition: Of less than normal height; below the average or normal level from which elevation is measured.
definitions.example: a low bow

definitions.definition: Not high in status, esteem or rank, dignity, or quality. (Compare vulgar.)
definitions.example: Now that was low even for you!

definitions.definition: Humble, meek, not haughty.
Disparaging; assigning little value or excellence.
definitions.example: She had a low opinion of cats. He took a low view of dogs.

definitions.definition: Being a nadir, a bottom.
definitions.example: the low point in her career

definitions.definition: Depressed in mood, dejected, sad.
definitions.example: low spirits

definitions.definition: Lacking health or vitality, strength or vivacity; feeble; weak.
definitions.example: a low pulse

definitions.definition: Small, not high (in amount or quantity, value, force, energy, etc).
definitions.example: My credit union charges a low interest rate.   Jogging during a whiteout, with such low temperatures and low visibility, is dangerous.   The store sold bread at low prices, and milk at even lower prices.   The contractors gave a low estimate of the costs.   low cholesterol   a low voltage wire   a low number

definitions.definition: Simple in complexity or development; (in several set phrases) favoring simplicity; (see e.g. low church, Low Tory).
definitions.example: low protozoan animals, low cryptogamic plants, and other low organisms

definitions.definition: (in several set phrases) Being near the equator.
definitions.example: the low northern latitudes

definitions.definition: (acoustics) Grave in pitch, due to being produced by relatively slow vibrations (wave oscillations); flat.
definitions.example: Generally, European men have lower voices than their Indian counterparts.

definitions.definition: Quiet; soft; not loud.
definitions.example: They spoke in low voices so I would not hear what they were saying.

definitions.definition: Made with a relatively large opening between the tongue and the palate; made with (part of) the tongue positioned low in the mouth, relative to the palate.
Lesser in value than other cards, denominations, suits, etc.
definitions.example: a low card

definitions.definition: Not rich or seasoned; offering the minimum of nutritional requirements; plain, simple.
(of an automobile, gear, etc) Designed for a slow (or the slowest) speed.
definitions.example: low gear

definitions.definition: Close to the ground.
Of a pitch, at a lower frequency.
With a low voice or sound; not loudly; gently.
definitions.example: to speak low

definitions.definition: Under the usual price; at a moderate price; cheaply.
definitions.example: He sold his wheat low.

definitions.definition: In a low mean condition; humbly; meanly.
In a time approaching our own.
In a path near the equator, so that the declination is small, or near the horizon, so that the altitude is small; said of the heavenly bodies with reference to the diurnal revolution.
definitions.example: The moon runs low, i.e. comparatively near the horizon when on or near the meridian.

definitions.definition: A spin in which the head and shoulders are dropped backwards and the back arched downwards toward the ice. Also called a layback spin
A maneuver to scale a vertical crack, by pulling with the arms and jamming the legs underneath, and shuffling up on limb at a time.
A surf maneuver where the upright surfer leans back against the wave.
A trick where the boarder leans back while on the lip of the ramp, or down a rail.
(audio editing) The process of recording the final sound onto the master track.
A short backwards pass to a teammate in an attacking position
A wedge-shaped kerbstone that allows a vehicle to enter a driveway without a bump
To climb using layback maneuvers.
Not ready.
The act of receiving, or the fact of having been received.
definitions.example: A balance payable on receipt of the goods.

definitions.definition: The fact of having received a blow, injury etc.
(in the plural) A quantity or amount received; takings.
definitions.example: This weekend's receipts alone cover our costs to mount the production!

definitions.definition: A written acknowledgment that a specified article or sum of money has been received.
(usually in the plural) (A piece of) evidence, documentation, etc. to prove one's past actions, accomplishments, etc.
(usually in the plural, by extension) (A piece of) evidence (e.g. documentation or screen captures) of past wrongdoing or problematic behavior or statements.
(archaic in New England and rural US since end of 20th century, elsewhere since middle of 20th century) A recipe, instructions, prescription.
A receptacle.
A revenue office.
Reception, as an act of hospitality.
Capability of receiving; capacity.
A recess; a retired place.
To give or write a receipt (for something).
definitions.example: to receipt delivered goods

definitions.definition: To put a receipt on, as by writing or stamping; to mark a bill as having been paid.
definitions.example: to receipt a bill

definitions.definition: A person who loves and zealously supports and defends their country.
A fellow countryman, a compatriot.
A solid, more or less rigid object of metal or wood with a uniform cross-section smaller than its length.
definitions.example: The window was protected by steel bars.

definitions.definition: A solid metal object with uniform (round, square, hexagonal, octagonal or rectangular) cross-section; in the US its smallest dimension is 1/4 inch or greater, a piece of thinner material being called a strip.
definitions.example: Ancient Sparta used iron bars instead of handy coins in more valuable alloy, to physically discourage the use of money.

definitions.definition: A cuboid piece of any solid commodity.
definitions.example: bar of chocolate

definitions.definition: A broad shaft, or band, or stripe.
definitions.example: a bar of colour

definitions.definition: A long, narrow drawn or printed rectangle, cuboid or cylinder, especially as used in a bar code or a bar chart.
Any of various lines used as punctuation or diacritics, such as the pipe ⟨|⟩, fraction bar (as in 12), and strikethrough (as in Ⱥ), formerly including oblique marks such as the slash.
The sign indicating that the characteristic of a logarithm is negative, conventionally placed above the digit(s) to show that it applies to the characteristic only and not to the mantissa.
A similar sign indicating that the charge on a particle is negative (and that consequently the particle is in fact an antiparticle).
A business licensed to sell alcoholic drinks for consumption on the premises, or the premises themselves; public house.
definitions.example: The street was lined with all-night bars.

definitions.definition: The counter of such premises.
definitions.example: Step up to the bar and order a drink.

definitions.definition: A counter, or simply a cabinet, from which alcoholic drinks are served in a private house or a hotel room.
(by extension, in combinations such as coffee bar, juice bar etc.) Premises or a counter serving any type of beverage.
An establishment where alcohol and sometimes other refreshments are served.
An informal establishment selling food to be consumed on the premises.
definitions.example: a burger bar

definitions.definition: An establishment offering cosmetic services.
definitions.example: a nail bar; a brow bar

definitions.definition: An official order or pronouncement that prohibits some activity.
definitions.example: The club has lifted its bar on women members.

definitions.definition: Anything that obstructs, hinders, or prevents; an obstruction; a barrier.
(whimsical, derived from fubar) A metasyntactic variable representing an unspecified entity, often the second in a series, following foo.
definitions.example: Suppose we have two objects, foo and bar.

definitions.definition: (Parliament) A dividing line (physical or notional) in the chamber of a legislature beyond which only members and officials may pass.
The railing surrounding the part of a courtroom in which the judges, lawyers, defendants and witnesses stay
"the Bar" or "the bar" The bar exam, the legal licensing exam.
definitions.example: He's studying hard to pass the Bar this time; he's failed it twice before.

definitions.definition: (metonym, "the Bar", "the bar") Collectively, lawyers or the legal profession; specifically applied to barristers in some countries but including all lawyers in others.
definitions.example: He was called to the bar, he became a barrister.

definitions.definition: One of an array of bar-shaped symbols that display the level of something, such as wireless signal strength or battery life remaining.
definitions.example: I don't have any bars in the middle of this desert.

definitions.definition: A vertical line across a musical staff dividing written music into sections, typically of equal durational value.
One of those musical sections.
A horizontal pole that must be crossed in high jump and pole vault
Any level of achievement regarded as a challenge to be overcome.
(most codes) The crossbar.
The central divider between the inner and outer table of a backgammon board, where stones are placed if they are hit.
An addition to a military medal, on account of a subsequent act
A linear shoaling landform feature within a body of water.
A ridge or succession of ridges of sand or other substance, especially a formation extending across the mouth of a river or harbor or off a beach, and which may obstruct navigation. (FM 55-501).
One of the ordinaries in heraldry; a fess.
A city gate, in some British place names.
definitions.example: Potter's Bar

definitions.definition: A drilling or tamping rod.
A vein or dike crossing a lode.
A gatehouse of a castle or fortified town.
The part of the crust of a horse's hoof which is bent inwards towards the frog at the heel on each side, and extends into the centre of the sole.
(in the plural) The space between the tusks and grinders in the upper jaw of a horse, in which the bit is placed.
To obstruct the passage of (someone or something).
definitions.example: Our way was barred by a huge rockfall.

definitions.definition: To prohibit.
definitions.example: I couldn't get into the nightclub because I had been barred.

definitions.definition: To lock or bolt with a bar.
definitions.example: bar the door

definitions.definition: To imprint or paint with bars, to stripe.
A non-SI unit of pressure equal to 100,000 pascals, approximately equal to atmospheric pressure at sea level.
(manner) In an equal manner; in equal shares or proportion; with equal and impartial justice; evenly
definitions.example: All citizens are equally taxed.

definitions.definition: (degree) In equal degree or extent; just as.
definitions.example: The gas stations are equally far from the highway.

definitions.definition: Used to link two or more coordinate elements
definitions.example: John suffered setbacks at his job. Equally, Frank's business slowed.

definitions.definition: Something that prevents, hampers, or hinders.
definitions.example: Age is often a handicap.

definitions.definition: An allowance of a certain amount of time or distance in starting, granted in a race (or other contest of skill) to the competitor possessing disadvantages; or an additional weight or other hindrance imposed upon the one possessing advantages, in order to equalize, as much as possible, the chances of success.
definitions.example: A handicap in chess often involves removal of the queen's rook.

definitions.definition: (sometimes considered offensive) The disadvantage itself, in particular physical or mental disadvantages of people.
A race or similar contest in which there is an allowance of time, distance, weight, or other advantage, to equalize the chances of the competitors.
(card game) An old card game, similar to lanterloo.
To encumber with a handicap in any contest.
(by extension) To place at disadvantage.
definitions.example: The candidate was handicapped by her lack of experience.

definitions.definition: To estimate betting odds.
definitions.example: Grandpa Andy would buy the racing form the day ahead of time so he could handicap the race before he even arrived at the track.

definitions.definition: A set of tasks that fulfills a purpose or duty; an assignment set by an employer, or by oneself.
Religious evangelism.
(in the plural, "the missions") third world charities, particularly those which preach as well as provide aid.
(Catholic tradition) an infrequent gathering of religious believers in a parish, usually part of a larger regional event with a central theme.
A number of people appointed to perform any service; a delegation; an embassy.
Dismissal; discharge from service
A settlement or building serving as a base for missionary work.
definitions.example: Many cities across the Americas grew from Spanish missions.

definitions.definition: To send on a mission.
Do missionary work, proselytize
The middle of the night: the sixth temporal hour, equidistant between sunset and sunrise.
12 o'clock at night exactly.
Utterly dark or black.
A sneaker or rubber overshoe.
A detective.
To act as a detective.
A type of game controller held in both hands and controlled with the thumbs.
Something given to another voluntarily, without charge.
A talent or natural ability.
definitions.example: She had a gift for playing the flute.

definitions.definition: Something gained incidentally, without effort.
The act, right, or power of giving or bestowing.
definitions.example: The office is in the gift of the President.

definitions.definition: To give as a gift or donation.
To give away, to concede easily.
To welcome in a friendly manner, either in person or through another means e.g. writing or over the phone/internet
To arrive at or reach, or meet (talking of something which brings joy)
To accost; to address.
To meet and give salutations.
To be perceived by (somebody).
definitions.example: A brilliant dawn greeted her eyes as she looked out of the window.

definitions.definition: To weep; to cry.
A conventional phrase used to start a letter or conversation or otherwise to acknowledge a person's arrival or presence.
definitions.example: It's polite to begin a letter with a greeting, but this practice is less common in email.

definitions.definition: The action of the verb to greet.
An economical notebook computer.
The area between the tee and the green, where the grass is cut short.
Any tract of land free from obstacles.
(Military) A channel either from offshore, in a river, or in a harbor that has enough depth to accommodate the draft of large vessels. (JP 4-01.6)
A navigable channel in a harbour, offshore etc; the usual course taken by vessels in such places.
Anything that is typical, normal, or standard.
definitions.example: Among the moths, typicals were more common than melanics.

definitions.definition: Capturing the overall sense of a thing.
Characteristically representing something by form, group, idea or type.
Normal, average; to be expected.
Of a lower taxon, containing the type of the higher taxon.
With respect to place; in place.
definitions.example: These things are locally separated.

definitions.definition: In or from the local area.
definitions.example: In this shop we only sell products farmed locally.

definitions.definition: In a restricted part of the body.
definitions.example: To be applied locally

definitions.definition: Within a sufficiently small sphere (or circle or interval) around a given point (sometimes, around any point).
definitions.example: locally Euclidean

definitions.definition: The quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable.
definitions.example: The Shakespearean Shylock is of dubious value in the modern world.

definitions.definition: The degree of importance given to something.
definitions.example: The value of my children's happiness is second only to that of my wife.

definitions.definition: That which is valued or highly esteemed, such as one's morals, morality, or belief system.
definitions.example: He does not share his parents' values.

definitions.definition: The amount (of money or goods or services) that is considered to be a fair equivalent for something else.
The relative duration of a musical note.
definitions.example: The value of a crotchet is twice that of a quaver.

definitions.definition: The relative darkness or lightness of a color in (a specific area of) a painting etc.
Any definite numerical quantity or other mathematical object, determined by being measured, computed, or otherwise defined.
definitions.example: The exact value of pi cannot be represented in decimal notation.

definitions.definition: Precise meaning; import.
definitions.example: the value of a word; the value of a legal instrument

definitions.definition: (in the plural) The valuable ingredients to be obtained by treating a mass or compound; specifically, the precious metals contained in rock, gravel, etc.
definitions.example: The vein carries good values.

definitions.definition: Esteem; regard.
Valour; also spelled valew.
To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.
definitions.example: I will have the family jewels valued by a professional.

definitions.definition: To fix or determine the value of; assign a value to, as of jewelry or art work.
To regard highly; think much of; place importance upon.
definitions.example: Gold was valued highly among the Romans.

definitions.definition: To hold dear.
definitions.example: I value these old photographs.

definitions.definition: A sudden transient rush, flood or increase.
definitions.example: He felt a surge of excitement.

definitions.definition: The maximum amplitude of a vehicle's forward/backward oscillation
A sudden electrical spike or increase of voltage and current.
definitions.example: A power surge at that generator created a blackout across the whole district.

definitions.definition: The swell or heave of the sea. (FM 55-501).
A spring; a fountain.
The tapered part of a windlass barrel or a capstan, upon which the cable surges, or slips.
To rush, flood, or increase suddenly.
definitions.example: Toaster sales surged last year.

definitions.definition: To accelerate forwards, particularly suddenly.
definitions.example: A ship surges forwards, sways sideways and heaves up.

definitions.definition: To slack off a line.
Covered in blotches.
The scientific study of plants, a branch of biology. Typically those disciplines that involve the whole plant.
The plant life of a geographical area; flora.
definitions.example: the botany of Greenland

definitions.definition: The properties and life phenomena exhibited by a plant, plant type, or plant group.
A botanical treatise or study, especially of a particular system of botany or that of a particular place.
A young offspring of a canid (ursid, felid, pinniped), especially of a dog or a wolf, the young of a bear or similar mammal (lion, tiger, seal); a pup, wolf cub.
An insolent youth; a mere child.
A kind of ship.
One of several wooden strips to prevent wear on a windlass on a clipper-era ship.
A tooth on a sprocket wheel (compare sprocket and cog).
The inedible parts of a grain-producing plant.
definitions.example: To separate out the chaff, early cultures tossed baskets of grain into the air and let the wind blow away the lighter chaff.

definitions.definition: Straw or hay cut up fine for the food of cattle.
Any excess or unwanted material, resource, or person; anything worthless.
Light jesting talk; banter; raillery.
Loose material, e.g. small strips of aluminum foil dropped from aircraft, intended to interfere with radar detection.
An opening, usually covered by one or more panes of clear glass, to allow light and air from outside to enter a building or vehicle.
An opening, usually covered by glass, in a shop which allows people to view the shop and its products from outside; a shop window.
The shutter, casement, sash with its fittings, or other framework, which closes a window opening.
A period of time when something is available.
definitions.example: launch window;  window of opportunity;  You have a two-hour window of clear weather to finish working on the lawn.

definitions.definition: A restricted range.

A rectangular area on a computer terminal or screen containing some kind of user interface, displaying the output of and allowing input for one of a number of simultaneously running computer processes.
A figure formed of lines crossing each other.
The time between first infection and detectability.
To furnish with windows.
To place at or in a window.
A reddish-brown, malleable, ductile metallic element with high electrical and thermal conductivity, symbol Cu, and atomic number 29.
The reddish-brown colour/color of copper.
Any of various specialized items that are made of copper, where the use of copper is either traditional or vital to the function of the item.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies with copper-coloured upperwings, especially those of the genera Lycaena and Paralucia.
To sheathe or coat with copper.
Made of copper.
Having the reddish-brown colour/color of copper.
In a royal manner; in a manner having to do with royalty.
definitions.example: Unless it was decreed royally, it never got done.

definitions.definition: Excessively; thoroughly.
definitions.example: He will be royally annoyed if you change his work.

definitions.definition: Easy to use, useful.
definitions.example: Some people regard duct tape as a handy fix-all.

definitions.definition: Nearby, within reach.
definitions.example: I keep a first-aid kit handy in case of emergency.

definitions.definition: Of a person: dexterous, skilful.
definitions.example: She's very handy: she made all her own kitchen cupboards.

definitions.definition: Physically violent; tending to use one's fists.
Of a freight ship: having a small cargo capacity (less than 40,000 DWT); belonging to the handysize class.
To refuse to allow
definitions.example: The prisoners were disallowed to contact with a lawyer.

definitions.definition: To reject as invalid, untrue, or improper
definitions.example: The goal was disallowed because the player was offside.

definitions.definition: Forbidden
Invalid
An area on a ship's deck or on the roof of a house used for sunbathing.
Consisting of scraps; fragmentary; lacking unity or consistency.
definitions.example: That was a scrappy lecture.

definitions.definition: Having an aggressive spirit; inclined to fight or strive.
definitions.example: He's a scrappy dog and will charge at you if you taunt him.

definitions.definition: (Of a fight) characterised by lots of ungainly or wild punches, grabs, wrestling, etc.
(Of a sports match) characterised by awkward or clumsy play.
A short spar, at the bows of a ship, that serves as a flagpole to fly a flag (a jack)
A chair with supports for the arms or elbows.
To create based on theory or general knowledge rather than data.
To theorize based on analysis of data that was gathered previously; to reflect.
Remote from actual involvement, including a person retired from previously active involvement.
definitions.example: These days I'm an armchair detective.

definitions.definition: Unqualified or uninformed but yet giving advice, especially on technical issues, such as law, architecture, medicine, military theory, or sports.
definitions.example: After the American football game, the armchair quarterbacks talked about what they would have done differently to win, if they had been star athletes instead of out-of-shape old men.

definitions.definition: The subatomic particle having a negative charge and orbiting the nucleus; the flow of electrons in a conductor constitutes electricity.
Alloys of magnesium and other metals, like aluminum or zinc, that were manufactured by the German company Chemische Fabrik Griesheim-Elektron.
The beginning of something.
The source of a river, information, goods, etc.
The point at which the axes of a coordinate system intersect.
The proximal end of attachment of a muscle to a bone that will not be moved by the action of that muscle.
An arbitrary point on Earth's surface, chosen as the zero for a system of coordinates.
(in the plural) Ancestry.
A person or thing that shakes, or by means of which something is shaken.
A variety of pigeon.
One who holds railroad spikes while they are hammered.
A musical percussion instrument filled with granular solids which produce a rhythmic sound when shaken.
A kind of straight-sided, stackable glass for beer, soda, etc.
Without awareness, without intent.
An older person or an older member, usually a leader, of some community.
definitions.example: We were presented to the village elder.

definitions.definition: One who is older than another.
definitions.example: Respect your elders.

definitions.definition: One who lived at an earlier period; a predecessor.
An officer of a church, sometimes having teaching responsibilities.
A clergyman authorized to administer all the sacraments.
definitions.example: a travelling elder

definitions.definition: One ordained to the lowest office in the Melchizedek priesthood.
definitions.example: After being a member of the Church for a while, Bill was ordained to the office of elder.

definitions.definition: Male missionary.
definitions.example: The elders are coming over for dinner tonight.

definitions.definition: (often capitalized) Title for a male missionary; title for a general authority.
definitions.example: One of the long-time leaders in the Church is Elder Packer.

definitions.definition: A pagan or Heathen priest or priestess.
To admonish or reprove for improper conduct by the elders of the meeting.
definitions.example: I was eldered for directly responding to someone else's message in meeting for worship.

definitions.definition: Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.
definitions.example: an old abandoned building;  an old friend

definitions.definition: Having been used and thus no longer new or unused.
definitions.example: I find that an old toothbrush is good to clean the keyboard with.

definitions.definition: Having existed or lived for the specified time.
definitions.example: How old are they? She’s five years old and he's seven. We also have a young teen and a two-year-old child.

definitions.definition: (heading) Of an earlier time.
Tiresome after prolonged repetition.
definitions.example: Your constant pestering is getting old.

definitions.definition: Said of subdued colors, particularly reds, pinks and oranges, as if they had faded over time.
A grammatical intensifier, often used in describing something positive. (Mostly in idioms like good old, big old and little old, any old and some old.)
definitions.example: We're having a good old time. My next car will be a big old SUV.  My wife makes the best little old apple pie in Texas.

definitions.definition: Excessive, abundant.
In female mammals, the organ in which the young are conceived and grow until birth; the uterus.
The abdomen or stomach.
The stomach of a person or creature.
A place where something is made or formed.
Any cavity containing and enveloping anything.
To enclose in a womb, or as if in a womb; to breed or hold in secret.
A more remote part of a town or city; the periphery, environs; a suburb.
definitions.example: Many people commute into the business district from the outskirts of town.

definitions.definition: To surround as an outskirt.
A rushing outward.
To rush outward; to issue forcibly.
To rush more than the other team.
One who is alone, lacking or avoiding the company of others.
definitions.example: I actually enjoy being a loner.

definitions.definition: To plan, control and record the position and course of a vehicle, ship, aircraft, etc., on a journey; to follow a planned course.
definitions.example: He navigated the bomber to the Ruhr.

definitions.definition: To give directions, as from a map, to someone driving a vehicle.
definitions.example: You drive. I'll navigate.

definitions.definition: To travel over water in a ship; to sail.
definitions.example: We navigated to France in the dinghy.

definitions.definition: To move between web pages, menus, etc. by means of hyperlinks, mouse clicks, or any other mechanism.
definitions.example: It was difficult to navigate back to the home page.

definitions.definition: To find a way through a difficult situation or process.
A hard external covering of an animal.
The hard calcareous covering of a bird egg.
One of the outer layers of skin of an onion.
definitions.example: The restaurant served caramelized onion shells.

definitions.definition: The hard external covering of various plant seed forms.
The accreted mineral formed around a hollow geode.
The casing of a self-contained single-unit artillery projectile.

A hollow, usually spherical or cylindrical projectile fired from a siege mortar or a smoothbore cannon. It contains an explosive substance designed to be ignited by a fuse or by percussion at the target site so that it will burst and scattered at high velocity its contents and fragments. Formerly called a bomb.
The cartridge of a breechloading firearm; a load; a bullet; a round.
Any slight hollow structure; a framework, or exterior structure, regarded as not complete or filled in, as the shell of a house.
A garment, usually worn by women, such as a shirt, blouse, or top, with short sleeves or no sleeves, that often fastens in the rear.
A coarse or flimsy coffin; a thin interior coffin enclosed within a more substantial one.
A string instrument, as a lyre, whose acoustical chamber is formed like a shell.
definitions.example: The first lyre may have been made by drawing strings over the underside of a tortoise shell.

definitions.definition: The body of a drum; the often wooden, often cylindrical acoustic chamber, with or without rims added for tuning and for attaching the drum head.
An engraved copper roller used in print works.
The thin coating of copper on an electrotype.
The watertight outer covering of the hull of a vessel, often made with planking or metal plating.
(rigging) The outer frame or case of a block within which the sheaves revolve.
A light boat whose frame is covered with thin wood, impermeable fabric, or water-proofed paper; a racing shell or dragon boat.
A set of atomic orbitals that have the same principal quantum number.
The outward form independent of what is inside.
The empty outward form of someone or something.
definitions.example: The setback left him a mere shell; he was never the same again.

definitions.definition: An emaciated person.
definitions.example: He's lost so much weight from illness; he's a shell of his former self.

definitions.definition: A psychological barrier to social interaction.
definitions.example: Even after months of therapy he's still in his shell.

definitions.definition: An operating system software user interface, whose primary purpose is to launch other programs and control their interactions; the user's command interpreter. Shell is a way to separate the internal complexity of the implementation of the command from the user. The internals can change while the user experience/interface remains the same.
definitions.example: The name "Bash" is an acronym which stands for "Bourne-again shell", itself a pun on the name of the "Bourne shell", an earlier Unix shell designed by Stephen Bourne, and the Christian concept of being "born again".

definitions.definition: A legal entity that has no operations.
definitions.example: A shell corporation was formed to acquire the old factory.

definitions.definition: A concave rough cast-iron tool in which a convex lens is ground to shape.
A gouge bit or shell bit.
The onset and coda of a syllable.
A person's ear.
definitions.example: Can I have a quick word in your shell?

definitions.definition: To remove the outer covering or shell of something.
To bombard, to fire projectiles at, especially with artillery.
To disburse or give up money, to pay. (Often used with out).
To fall off, as a shell, crust, etc.
To cast the shell, or exterior covering; to fall out of the pod or husk.
definitions.example: Nuts shell in falling.

definitions.definition: To switch to a shell or command line.
To form shallow, irregular cracks (in a coating).
To form a shelling.
Beginning; start.
To start, to initiate or take the first step into something.
definitions.example: I began playing the piano at the age of five.   Now that everyone is here, we should begin the presentation.

definitions.definition: To be in the first stage of some situation
definitions.example: The program begins at 9 o'clock on the dot.    I rushed to get to class on time, but the lesson had already begun.

definitions.definition: To come into existence.
Persistent attacks and criticism causing worry and distress.
Deliberate pestering or annoying.
Excessive intimidation.
An act of capturing; a seizing by force or stratagem.
The securing of an object of strife or desire, as by the power of some attraction.
definitions.example: the capture of a lover's heart

definitions.definition: Something that has been captured; a captive.
The recording or storage of something for later playback.
definitions.example: video capture

definitions.definition: A particular match found for a pattern in a text string.
To take control of; to seize by force or stratagem.
definitions.example: to capture an enemy, a vessel, or a criminal

definitions.definition: To store (as in sounds or image) for later revisitation.
definitions.example: She captured the details of the fresco in a series of photographs.

definitions.definition: To reproduce convincingly.
definitions.example: His film adaptation captured the spirit of the original work.

definitions.definition: To remove or take control of an opponent’s piece in a game (e.g., chess, go, checkers).
definitions.example: He captured his opponent’s queen on the 15th move.

definitions.definition: An animal of the family Felidae:
A person:
A strong tackle used to hoist an anchor to the cathead of a ship.
Short form of cat-o'-nine-tails.
A sturdy merchant sailing vessel (now only in "catboat").
The game of "trap and ball" (also called "cat and dog").
The pointed piece of wood that is struck in the game of tipcat.
A vagina, a vulva; the female external genitalia.
A double tripod (for holding a plate, etc.) with six feet, of which three rest on the ground, in whatever position it is placed.
A wheeled shelter, used in the Middle Ages as a siege weapon to allow assailants to approach enemy defences.
To hoist (the anchor) by its ring so that it hangs at the cathead.
To flog with a cat-o'-nine-tails.
To vomit.
To go wandering at night.
To gossip in a catty manner.
A program and command in Unix that reads one or more files and directs their content to the standard output.
To apply the cat command to (one or more files).
To dump large amounts of data on (an unprepared target) usually with no intention of browsing it carefully.
A catapult.
definitions.example: a carrier's bow cats

definitions.definition: Any of a variety of earth-moving machines. (from their manufacturer Caterpillar Inc.)
A caterpillar drive vehicle (a ground vehicle which uses caterpillar tracks), especially tractors, trucks, minibuses, and snow groomers.
A twin-hulled ship or boat.
A quarrelsome woman; a scold.
A raft of three pieces of wood lashed together, the middle piece being longer than the others, and serving as a keel on which the rower squats while paddling.
An old kind of fireship.
A group, often named or numbered, to which items are assigned based on similarity or defined criteria.
definitions.example: I wouldn't put this book in the same category as the author's first novel.

definitions.definition: A collection of objects, together with a transitively closed collection of composable arrows between them, such that every object has an identity arrow, and such that arrow composition is associative.
definitions.example: Just as a monoid consists of an underlying set with a binary operation "on top of it" which is closed, associative and with an identity, a category consists of an underlying digraph with an arrow composition operation "on top of it" which is transitively closed, associative, and with an identity at each object. In fact, a category's composition operation, when restricted to a single one of its objects, turns that object's set of arrows (which would all be loops) into a monoid.

definitions.definition: Any fish of the order Siluriformes, mainly found in fresh water, lacking scales, and having barbels like whiskers around the mouth
Someone who creates a fake profile on a social media platform in order to deceive people.
Such a fake profile.
Of or pertaining to a vector.
In the electron transport chain, being or relating to a type of enzyme-mediated reaction that moves protons in a specific direction (from the matrix into the inter-membrane space in this example).
An implement for cracking nuts.
Either of two birds of the genus Nucifraga in the crow family.
A bootleg mixed drink made from a blend of alcohols and fruit juices.
To take or to test a sample or samples of.
To reduce a continuous signal (such as a sound wave) to a discrete signal.
To reuse a portion of (an existing sound recording) in a new piece of music.
To make or show something similar to a sample.
The process or technique of obtaining a representative sample.
A sample.
The analysis of a group by determining the characteristics of a significant percentage of its members chosen at random.
The measurement, at regular intervals, of the amplitude of a varying waveform in order to convert it to digital form.
Electronically splicing pieces of previously recorded sound as part of a composition, especially as part of hip-hop or electronic dance music.
An injured, infected, inflamed or diseased patch of skin.
definitions.example: They put ointment and a bandage on the sore.

definitions.definition: Grief; affliction; trouble; difficulty.
To mutilate the legs or feet of (a horse) in order to induce a particular gait.
Causing pain or discomfort; painfully sensitive.
definitions.example: Her feet were sore from walking so far.

definitions.definition: Sensitive; tender; easily pained, grieved, or vexed; very susceptible of irritation.
Dire; distressing.
definitions.example: The school was in sore need of textbooks, theirs having been ruined in the flood.

definitions.definition: Feeling animosity towards someone; annoyed or angered.
definitions.example: Joe was sore at Bob for beating him at checkers.

definitions.definition: Criminal; wrong; evil.
Very, excessively, extremely (of something bad).
Sorely.
Not sanitary; unhealthy; dirty.
To brood, raise, or maintain eggs, organisms, or living tissue through the provision of ideal environmental conditions.
To incubate metaphorically; to ponder an idea slowly and deliberately as if in preparation for hatching it.
A North American plover (Charadrius vociferus) with a distinctive cry and territorial behavior that includes feigning injury to distract interlopers from the nest.
